name: CCCS of Northeastern Pennsylvania
description_short: CCCS of Northeastern PA is a nonprofit credit counseling agency offering free debt management, credit counseling, and bankruptcy education services via phone, online, and in-person.
description_long:
CCCS of Northeastern Pennsylvania is a nonprofit credit counseling organization based in Pittston, PA, serving the northeastern region. The organization has established itself as a provider of financial guidance and debt management solutions for consumers struggling with unsecured debt and financial challenges.

The organization offers a comprehensive suite of free and fee-based services including credit counseling (described as 100% free and confidential), debt management programs, credit report reviews, housing counseling, and bankruptcy-related services. They provide bankruptcy pre-filing counseling and debtor education classes required by bankruptcy courts. Their online credit counseling system operates 24/7 and is described as the first system of its kind to provide personalized counseling advice online. They also operate an online payment center (Payer Express) for existing debt management program clients.

CCCS of NEPA distinguishes itself through multiple service delivery channels—telephone, in-person at their Pittston location, and fully online. Their free credit counseling and budget-setting services appear designed for consumers at various financial stages. The organization emphasizes confidentiality and accessibility, with their online platform available continuously and described as secure. They maintain a Client Bill of Rights and published privacy policy.

The organization is a legitimate nonprofit serving genuine financial counseling needs. However, the website provides limited detail about specific program costs, success rates, timeline to debt freedom, or creditor negotiation terms within debt management programs. Consumers would need to contact directly via phone (866-699-2227) to understand fee structures and program details. The focus on debt management programs suggests they may benefit financially from enrollment, though credit counseling itself is explicitly free.
pros: Credit counseling services are explicitly 100% free and confidential
24/7 online credit counseling available from home without appointment
Multiple service channels: online, phone (866-699-2227), and in-person at Pittston location
Bankruptcy counseling and education classes—required services for filers
Credit report review service offered to assess financial situation
Housing counseling available beyond just debt management
Published Client Bill of Rights and Privacy Policy demonstrate transparency commitment
Online payment center (Payer Express) for debt management program clients
cons: Website does not disclose specific fees for debt management programs or other non-free services
No information about creditor negotiation success rates or average debt reduction timelines
Limited details on program eligibility requirements or debt amount thresholds
No client testimonials or outcomes data publicly available on website
Website content appears to be from 2018 (copyright notice), raising currency questions
best_for: Consumers seeking free budget counseling and money management education without enrollment pressure
Individuals required to complete pre-bankruptcy counseling and debtor education for court filings
Debt management program candidates with unsecured debt who want nonprofit guidance
Pennsylvania/NEPA residents seeking in-person or phone-based credit counseling
diagnosis: CCCS of NEPA is best for consumers in Pennsylvania seeking free, confidential credit counseling or those requiring court-mandated bankruptcy counseling. The primary caveat is that while initial credit counseling is free, debt management programs involve unstated fees and lack published details about creditor negotiation rates, timeline to payoff, or success metrics—requiring direct phone contact (866-699-2227) to understand program economics.
services: Free credit counseling (100% free and confidential)
Debt management program enrollment and management
Bankruptcy pre-filing counseling (online, phone, or in-person)
Online payment center for debt management clients (Payer Express)
Personal financial management resources and FAQs
